retrieved_docs.title: Zaleplon -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Insomnia is a highly prevalent disorder in the US and worldwide. The definition of insomnia is the presence of "long sleep latency, frequent nocturnal awakenings, or prolonged periods of wakefulness during the sleep period or even frequent transient arousals." [1] Another definition describes insomnia disorder as the 'subjective report of difficulty with sleep initiation, duration, consolidation, or quality which occurs despite having an adequate opportunity for sleep and resulting in some form of daytime impairment. According to the fifth edition of the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-5) (American Psychiatric Association 2013) and the third edition of the International Classification of Sleep Disorders (ICSD-3) (American Academy of Sleep Medicine 2014), dissatisfaction with sleep quantity or quality has to occur at least three nights per week over at least three months to merit a diagnosis of chronic insomnia. [2] These criteria differ from the previous classification by considering the frequency criterion and the increase in the duration of symptoms from one to three months.

retrieved_docs.title: Median -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The median is the middle value in a set of numbers and is equivalent to the 50th percentile. In other words, the median is the midpoint of a set of numbers, with half of the values less than the median and half above it. [1] [2] [3] [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Confabulation -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Confabulation is a neuropsychiatric disorder wherein a patient generates a false memory without the intention of deceit. [1] The patient believes the statement to be truthful, hence the descriptive term “honest lying.” [2] The hypothesis is that the patient generates information as a compensatory mechanism to fill holes in one’s memories. [3] It functions for self-coherence, integration of memories, and self-relevance. [4] [1]  Confabulations can include small details such as birthdays, or they may be fantastical and more broadly based. [5] They can be believable or bizarre. Presenting patients with contradictory information may further perpetuate confabulation in an attempt to explain their account.

retrieved_docs.title: Median -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: What should be done if the set contains an even number of values? In this case, the median is the average of the 2 middle numbers. To illustrate this principle, the second set of numbers (7, 3, 10, 2, 9, 2, 1, 4) should be considered. After arranging the set of numbers in ascending order, we get (1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 7, 9, 10). As the second set of numbers has 8 numbers, the 2 middle values are 3 and 4. The median is the average of these 2 numbers, which is calculated as follows: (3+4)/2 = 3.5 Notably, the median of 3.5 is different from the mean, or average, of the set of numbers, which is 4.75. This is calculated as follows: (1+2+2+3+4+7+9+10)/8 = 38/8 = 4.75

retrieved_docs.title: Lensmaker’s Equation -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The lensmaker equation is a mathematical computation to determine the focal length of a lens in air. The focal length of a lens is defined as the distance from the center of the lens to an optical point to which all light rays are refracted. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Myers-Briggs Type Indicator -- Definition/Introduction -- Judging
retrieved_docs.content: Judging categorizes how individuals prefer to make decisions, ranging from thinking to feeling. Thinkers base their decisions on logic and facts, while feelers prioritize harmony in resolving issues.

retrieved_docs.title: HCQM Two-Midnight Rule -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The level of care provided to patients is not a one-size-fits-all approach but one that varies with the clinical presentation and the clinician's professional judgment regarding the expected treatment course. It is also essential for healthcare staff to understand that the "two-midnight rule" primarily applies to Medicare beneficiaries; private payers have their own screening criteria to determine the appropriate level of care.

retrieved_docs.title: Lambda Waves -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: As electrodes are not in direct contact with the signal generator (conduction volume phenomenon), brain activity, even localized, can appear widely dispersed on the scalp, and any brain event can be reflected in more than 1 site on the scalp. As a general rule, 50 percent of the signal recorded by a sensor positioned on the leather arises from the brain tissue immediately below that sensor, while the remaining signal is received from other locations, primarily from adjacent sites. Consequently, the EEG represents an exam that has poor localization ability. However, technological advances, such as high-density EEG, have allowed more localization information to be obtained from the EEG.

retrieved_docs.title: Lung Sounds -- Definition/Introduction -- Adventitious Sounds
retrieved_docs.content: The first trait that assists in the classification of adventitious sounds is whether the sounds are continuous or intermittent. For example, rhonchi and wheezes are continuous sounds, whereas crackles are not. Crackles could be counted by the examiner as discrete acoustic events [<25ms, interrupted, like dropping a marble on the floor], whereas rhonchi and wheezes are somewhat inseparable noise [>250ms, constant, like the whirring of a fan]. The next thing to note is the pitch:  wheezes and fine crackles are high-pitched, whereas rhonchi and coarse crackles are low-pitched

retrieved_docs.title: Mode -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The mode refers to the most frequently occurring number found in a group of numbers. The mode is determined by collecting data to count the frequency of each result. The result with the highest count of occurrences is known as the mode of the set, which is also commonly referred to as the modal value.  If multiple results all have the same high number of counts, then there can be multiple modes in the data set.

retrieved_docs.title: Algor Mortis -- Definition/Introduction -- Definition
retrieved_docs.content: Determining the time of death is crucial in forensic investigations. Algor mortis  is one of the earliest and most widely used phenomena to estimate the time since death or postmortem interval (PMI). This phenomenon is one of the three cardinal signs of death, the other ones being livor mortis and rigor mortis. Algor mortis is frequently mentioned in forensic literature and portrayed in the media; however, its accuracy as a PMI indicator varies greatly. The physiological underpinnings of algor mortis, variables affecting body cooling, and the constraints of its forensic uses—particularly in refrigerated and cold environments—are examined in this activity. Furthermore, novel research approaches and technological developments are required to enhance the precision of PMI estimation.

retrieved_docs.title: Rigler Sign -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: In 1942, American radiologist Leo G Rigler (1896-1979) described the Rigler sign for pneumoperitoneum based on patterns observed in 4 cases reported in 1941. He noted that this sign was present when large quantities of free air were in the peritoneal cavity. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Lung Sounds -- Definition/Introduction -- Adventitious Sounds
retrieved_docs.content: A rub is a grating sound coming from an inflamed pleura rubbing against one another. It is usually louder than other lung sounds due to its generation closer to the chest wall. Rubs usually occur during both inspiration and expiration at a mirrored point in the respiratory cycle. [5] Rubs are most often confused with crackles but are distinguished by the rub's biphasic, localized quality, often with overlying point pain on the chest wall.

retrieved_docs.title: Myers-Briggs Type Indicator -- Definition/Introduction -- Energy
retrieved_docs.content: Energy refers to the spectrum of extraversion to introversion. Individuals leaning toward extraversion focus their attention on external experiences and actions, drawing energy from their interactions with others. In contrast, those leaning toward introversion concentrate on their inner thoughts and ideas, gaining energy from solitude.

retrieved_docs.title: Ghon Complex -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Cavitation of Ghon complexes occurs with greater frequency in children when compared with adults, and the presence of these lesions correlates with positive gastric aspirates and sputum cultures in children. [1] It is thought that the propensity of the Ghon complex to cavitate may also be responsible for the increased incidence of extrapulmonary tuberculosis in children. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Median -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: A short example will help clarify this point. In the set of 7 numbers (8, 6, 9, 5, 8, 23, 4), the median is found by first sorting the numbers from lowest to highest (in ascending order). The sorted set becomes (4, 5, 6, 8, 8, 9, 23). The median is the middle number. As there are 7 numbers in the set, the fourth value, '8', is the median. Notably, the median differs from the mean. The mean is the "average" of the values in the set, calculated as follows: (4+5+6+8+8+9+23)/7 = 63/7 = 9

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2320
retrieved_docs.content: The gestational period of 29‑30 weeks completed is directly linked to the outcome of a liveborn infant, indicating that reaching this stage of pregnancy can result in a newborn who is alive at birth. In the broader classification of pregnancy timelines, the concept of Duration of gestation encompasses liveborn as a specific category or outcome, reflecting that livebirth is considered one of the possible results within the overall spectrum of gestational durations. This relationship underscores the clinical relevance of gestational age in determining the likelihood of a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 1983
retrieved_docs.content: The concept of “Duration of gestation” is classified within the International Statistical Classification of Diseases and Related Health Problems, 10th Revision, Australian Modification (ICD‑10‑AM), indicating that gestational length is a recognized medical category in this coding system. Within this framework, the specific gestational interval of “29‑30 weeks of gestation completed” is linked to the outcome of a “liveborn” infant, reflecting that infants born after completing 29 to 30 weeks of pregnancy can be alive at birth. Accordingly, the “liveborn” outcome is treated as a distinct subclass or child concept under the broader umbrella of gestational duration, illustrating how gestational age ranges and birth outcomes are organized together in clinical classification and patient‑care documentation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2303
retrieved_docs.content: The category “Other conditions or status of the mother complicating pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium” includes, as a child concept, the specific condition “Uterine size for dates discrepancy.” This specific condition is also classified as a “Finding related to pregnancy,” indicating that uterine size for dates discrepancy is both a maternal condition that can complicate pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium and a particular type of pregnancy‑related clinical finding.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 1 -- Community 263
retrieved_docs.content: The medical concepts described are tightly linked through hierarchical and associative relationships that clarify how gestational timing, perinatal disorders, and reproductive health services intersect. The specific gestational interval “29‑30 weeks of gestation completed” is connected to the outcome category “liveborn,” indicating that a liveborn infant can result from pregnancies reaching this stage. Conversely, the broader classification “Disorder relating to short gestation AND/OR low birthweight” lists “liveborn” as its parent, positioning liveborn as a more general category that encompasses such disorders. “Duration of gestation” is defined as an inverse‑isa of “liveborn,” meaning that the liveborn status is a particular instance within the overall spectrum of gestational durations. Encounters with health services for reproductive reasons (ICD‑10 Z30‑Z39) also cite “liveborn” as a parent, reflecting that liveborn infants are a key focus of reproductive health care encounters. The

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 1 -- Community 131
retrieved_docs.content: **Diabetes or abnormal glucose tolerance complicating pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium**. **Cervical shortening in pregnancy** is classified as an **abnormality of the cervix (obstetric)** and is a child of both **Other conditions or status of the mother complicating pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium** and **Other complications of birth; puerperium affecting management of the mother**. It is further divided into **Cervical shortening, second trimester** and **Cervical shortening, third trimester**, reflecting its temporal presentation. Cervical shortening is also regarded as a form of **early or threatened labor** and is associated with **short cervical length in pregnancy** and **cervical insufficiency** (Cervixinsufficiëntie als indicatie voor zorg bij moeder), which are considered indications for maternal care when pelvic‑organ abnormalities are known or suspected. **Uterine size for dates discrepancy** is a child of **Other conditions or status of the mother

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 215
retrieved_docs.content: cervix (obstetric)” and are linked to “Early or threatened labor” (Arabic “مخاض مهدد”). The second‑trimester form of cervical shortening is explicitly mapped to “Early or threatened labor,” while the unspecified‑trimester form is also associated with “Short cervical length in pregnancy.” These cervical conditions are further connected to the indication hierarchy “Indicaties voor zorg bij moeder wegens bekende of vermoede afwijking van bekkenorganen,” which itself is a child of “Uteruslitteken van vroegere operatie als indicatie voor zorg bij moeder” (uterine scar from prior cesarean section, Arabic “عملية قيصرية سابقة,” term “ندبة رحمية”). This uterine‑scar concept is a key “Indication for care in pregnancy; labor; and delivery” and relates to “maternal care for other type of scar from previous cesarean delivery.” Other maternal status findings include “Uterine size for dates discrepancy” (a “Finding related to pregnancy” occurring during the maternal pregnancy period) and “Spotting

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 4 -- Community 2865
retrieved_docs.content: Duration of gestation refers to the length of time a fetus remains in the uterus before birth, and the classification of a birth as liveborn is encompassed within this gestational timeframe; therefore, liveborn status is defined by the specific duration of gestation at delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2850
retrieved_docs.content: Newborn Screening Terminology is a defined set of terms that encompasses the concept plod and also includes the concept الولادة بالملقط, indicating that both of these items are part of its subset of screening-related terminology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 2 -- Community 1156
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening in pregnancy is categorized under the broader group of other complications of birth; puerperium affecting management of mother and is recognized as an obstetric abnormality of the cervix. This condition encompasses three specific subtypes: Cervical shortening, second trimester; Cervical shortening, third trimester; and Cervical shortening, unspecified trimester. Each of these subtypes is classified as early or threatened labor, indicating that a shortened cervical length at any stage of pregnancy can herald the onset of labor before term. The unspecified‑trimester subtype is also directly mapped to the concept of short cervical length in pregnancy, reinforcing its clinical definition. In addition to cervical shortening, Stenosis of cervix in pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium is another obstetric cervical abnormality, illustrating that multiple cervical pathologies can arise during gestation and the peripartum period. Together, these entities highlight the importance of assessing cervical length and integrity throughout pregnancy, as alterations such as shortening or stenosis are linked to the risk of premature or threatened labor and require careful management to optimize maternal and fetal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 1940
retrieved_docs.content: Fetal monitoring is classified as تنظيم الجنين.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 4 -- Community 2763
retrieved_docs.content: Short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation constitute a severe perinatal condition that is classified as an extremely premature birth of the highest degree with unspecified weight (ولادة بأقصى درجات الابتسار، غير محددة {الوزن}). This classification highlights that the infant is born far before term, exhibits markedly reduced body weight, and shows evidence of impaired intra‑uterine growth, all of which place the newborn at high risk for morbidity and mortality and necessitate intensive neonatal monitoring and intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 1 -- Community 202
retrieved_docs.content: The medical framework links gestational age, birth outcomes, and perinatal disorders through a hierarchical classification system. A gestational period of 29‑30 weeks completed is identified as a precursor to a liveborn outcome, indicating that infants born after this duration can survive outside the womb. Duration of gestation is positioned as a broader category that inversely encompasses liveborn, underscoring that the length of pregnancy defines the viability of a live birth. Conditions associated with short gestation and low birth weight — collectively described as Short gestation; low birth weight; and fetal growth retardation — serve as a primary classifier for liveborn infants, reflecting the clinical importance of prematurity and insufficient fetal growth in determining neonatal status. This same condition is also classified as ولادة بأقصى درجات الابتسار، غير محددة {الوزن} (“birth at the highest degree of prematurity, unspecified weight”), reinforcing its role as a marker of

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2233
retrieved_docs.content: Normal pregnancy and/or delivery is categorized under several specific types of quadruplet gestations. It is classified as حمل بأربعة توائم، تمت الولادة, indicating a pregnancy with four fetuses that has resulted in delivery. It is also classified as حمل بأربعة توائم، غير محدد لنوبة الرعاية and حمل بأربعة توائم، غير محددة لنوبة الرعاية, both describing a quadruplet pregnancy that has not been assigned to a particular care episode. In addition, Normal pregnancy and/or delivery is linked to the broader concept of حمل متعدد, reflecting that it encompasses multiple‑pregnancy scenarios. This classification framework helps clinicians recognize the various presentations of quadruplet pregnancies and their relationship to overall multiple‑pregnancy care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 198
retrieved_docs.content: The gestational interval of 29‑30 weeks completed is linked to the outcome of a liveborn infant, and the disorder “Disorder relating to short gestation AND/OR low birthweight” has liveborn as its parent, making liveborn a central node that connects specific gestational ages with related perinatal disorders. Duration of gestation is a broader category that includes liveborn (inverse isa), and the combined description “Short gestation; low birth weight; and fetal growth retardation” classifies liveborn, underscoring that premature delivery, low birth weight, and fetal growth restriction are key classifications of liveborn status. The qualifier Neonatal (qualifier value) functions as a subclass of the overarching category Fetal and/or neonatal period (inverse isa) and qualifies a range of conditions that occur in the neonatal period. Recorded occurrences qualified by Neonatal include Bacteriële sepsis van pasgeborene, niet gespecificeerd, Disorder relating to short gestation AND/OR low

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 151
retrieved_docs.content: (unspecified condition originating in the perinatal period), situating fetal growth restriction within a larger, undefined perinatal condition category. Together, these relationships illustrate how gestational duration, low birth weight, fetal growth restriction, and associated perinatal disorders are organized within medical classification systems, aiding clinicians in diagnosing and managing newborns with short gestation and related complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is classified as a subcategory within the broader group of Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium, indicating that these health issues are primarily associated with the gestational period and its immediate aftermath.
